---
title: "Paper I — Geometry First"
description: >-
  Establishes the discrete bipartite octahedral lattice as the
  ontological substrate of the A=1 Discrete Causal Lattice series
  and recovers Lorentz invariance as an O_h-averaged consequence.
draft: true
---

<!--
  Draft landing page for Paper I. Does not render to the live site
  while `draft: true` is set.

  Conventions:
    - Lift the abstract verbatim from the deposited paper (v1.0).
    - Updates only when the paper version changes. The framing
      paragraph here is the *canonical on-site abstract* — it should
      agree with the Zenodo deposit's abstract field.
    - When ready to publish, see `notes/seo_next_steps.md` on
      whether to add a `citation:` block (Scholar-canonical
      decision — Zenodo is currently canonical).
-->

## Abstract

<!-- Verbatim from the Zenodo deposit's abstract. -->

## What this paper establishes

- **Substrate.** <One paragraph on the bipartite octahedral lattice,
  the A=1 conservation invariant, and the discrete bipartite tick
  rule.>
- **Lorentz invariance.** <One paragraph on the O_h-averaging result
  that recovers continuum Lorentz from the discrete substrate.>

## Methodological role

First step in the methodological arc: *geometry first → geometry
forces physics → geometry axiomatizes physics*. <One paragraph
locating Paper I as the substrate-priority paper that the rest of
the series builds on.>

## Audit-row impact

<List the audit rows that flipped to **PASS** in this paper, one
per line with the row identifier and a one-clause rationale. PART
/ STUB / FAIL rows below, same shape.>

## Open questions deferred downstream

<What this paper leaves open and which downstream paper picks it
up. Examples: Standard Model gauge derivation → Paper II; quantum
Roche limit → Paper III; discrete-probability prefactor → planned.>

## Citation and version

- **Version:** v1.0
- **DOI:** [10.5281/zenodo.20078529](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.20078529)
- **Repository:** [JackDMenendez/dcl](https://github.com/JackDMenendez/dcl)
- **GitHub release:** [v1.0](https://github.com/JackDMenendez/dcl/releases/tag/v1.0)

The canonical citation lives on the Zenodo deposit page; export
BibTeX / RIS / CSL from there.
